notes: Normal mode row

The Spiky Ball is a consumable ranged weapon that is primarily used as a passive trap by tossing them to the ground, where enemies who pass over them will take damage up to 7 times (after which the Spiky Balls vanish). If not destroyed, they will automatically vanish after 1 minute and 20 seconds. [ 1 ] They can also be directly thrown at enemies, but they have a very short arc and quickly fall to the ground. Spiky Balls have a 199/400 (49.75%) chance of being dropped by all goblins during the Goblin Army event (except for the Goblin Warlock ), and can be purchased from the Goblin Tinkerer for 80 CC each.

conditions: The Spiky Ball Trap is a type of trap that can be found naturally in the Jungle Temple and, rarely, outside the Temple's door. When activated, it dispenses orange spiky balls that bounce several times. It has a cooldown of (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) 5 / (Old-gen console and 3DS versions) 4 seconds between activations. The spiky balls deal 80 / 160 / 240 damage to players (40 damage to NPCs , enemies , and critters ). [ 1 ]

notes: Normal mode row

The Squirrel Hook can be purchased from the Zoologist for 2 GC after filling out at least 10% (55 entries) of the Bestiary . Therefore, it is normally always available for purchase, since the Zoologist does not appear before the Bestiary has been filled to at least 10%. The only exception is the secret world seed Vampirism , where she is the starting NPC .

conditions: The Staff of Regrowth is a tool that can be found in Ivy Chests as well as Jungle and Bramble Crates . When harvesting blooming stage herbs , it increases the yield of herbs and their seeds . The Staff of Regrowth can also be used on Dirt Blocks to plant grass without the need for Grass Seeds . Unlike other tools, it will not break vegetation when swung, unless directly targeting the tile. The Staff of Regrowth will automatically replant harvested herbs placed in a Clay Pot or a Planter Box .

The Staff of Regrowth is a tool that can be found in Ivy Chests as well as Jungle and Bramble Crates . When harvesting blooming stage herbs , it increases the yield of herbs and their seeds . The Staff of Regrowth can also be used on Dirt Blocks to plant grass without the need for Grass Seeds . Unlike other tools, it will not break vegetation when swung, unless directly targeting the tile. The Staff of Regrowth will automatically replant harvested herbs placed in a Clay Pot or a Planter Box .

notes: Normal mode row
The Star Hairpin is a vanity item that can be purchased from the Traveling Merchant for 2 GC . It is worn in the helmet slot.

The Static Hook is a Hardmode grappling hook that fires up to two hooks which can be launched simultaneously, reaching up to 37.5 tiles (the longest reach of any hook). The Static Hook can be purchased from the Steampunker for 50 GC .

The Steampunk Boiler is a Hardmode crafting station used to craft Steampunk furniture out of Cogs . The Steampunk Boiler can be purchased from the Steampunker for 10 GC , once the Eye of Cthulhu , Eater of Worlds / Brain of Cthulhu and Skeletron have been defeated.
Normal mode row
Normal mode row

conditions: The Stinger is a crafting material used in various Jungle-themed crafting recipes. It is obtained from Spiked Jungle Slimes , Hornets , and Moss Hornets found in the Underground Jungle biome .
The Stinkbug is a harmless critter which spawns on the surface . The Stinkbug has only 5 health and no defense , and can be damaged by other enemies and players.

Stinkfish are a type of fish which are found rarely by fishing in any biome in any layer, with the exception of The Corruption , The Hallow and (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) the Desert . They are used to craft Seafood Dinners and Stink Potions .

The Stone Block is a type of block that is generated naturally in very large parts of the world. The majority of the Underground and Cavern layers is comprised of Stone Blocks. As a crafting ingredient, they are vital early in the game to craft the Furnace and Wooden Arrows .

notes: The Stopwatch can be purchased from the Traveling Merchant for 5 GC .

conditions: The Desert Tiger Staff is always found in the Dungeon 's Desert Chest , which can only be unlocked with a Desert Key after Plantera has been defeated.

notes: The Strange Brew is a pre-Hardmode recovery potion that can be purchased from the Skeleton Merchant during certain moon phases for 5 SC . When used, it restores 70–120 health , inflicts 40–70 seconds of Potion Sickness , and has a 3/5 (60%) chance to grant up to 4 seconds of invincibility . The duration of Potion Sickness can be reduced via the Philosopher's Stone or Charm of Myths .
sourceRefName: Skeleton Merchant during certain moon phases for

notes: The Strange Glowing Mushroom is a Hardmode pet-summoning item that spawns a Baby Truffle that follows the player. The Baby Truffle emits a small amount of blue light similar to Glowing Mushrooms . The Strange Glowing Mushroom can be purchased from the Truffle for 45 GC .

The Stynger has a 1/7 (14.29%) chance of being dropped by Golem . When dropped, the Stynger is always accompanied by 60 to 99 Stynger Bolts. Additional Stynger Bolts can be purchased from the Witch Doctor and Arms Dealer ( 75 CC per bolt), if the Stynger is in the player's inventory .
The Summer Hat is a vanity item equipped in the Social Helmet Slot. It is purchased from the Clothier for 1 GC , when he is spoken to during the day outside of a Graveyard .

The Sunflower is a large, tall plant which can be found growing naturally on grass , and can be broken with a pickaxe or drill , or by coming into contact with water . It cannot be grown from seeds like most other plants, but is instead placed fully-grown, either naturally upon world generation or by the player. It can also be purchased from the Dryad for 50 SC each, while a Blood Moon is not occurring.

conditions: Sunflowers grant the Happy! buff to players in their vicinity, increasing their movement speed and reducing enemy spawns. Furthermore, they serve as a small barrier against the spread of the Corruption / Crimson and Hallow , negate some of the effects of the evil biomes, and help preventing Graveyard biomes from forming.

The Super Dart Trap is a trap found inside the Jungle Temple and, rarely, outside the Temple's door. It fires a dart when activated via Wire , which costs no ammunition , but has a cooldown period of (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) 3.33 seconds / (Old-gen console and 3DS versions) 3 seconds between firings. The darts deal 80 / 160 / 240 damage [ 1 ] and inflict Poisoned for 15 / 30 / 37.5 seconds to players (40 damage and 15 seconds of Poisoned to NPCs , enemies , and critters ). [ 2 ]

In addition to being crafted, Suspicious Looking Eyes also have a 1/5 (20%) chance of being found in Ivy Chests and Gold Chests found in the Cavern layer , including locked ones found inside the Dungeon .

notes: Normal mode row
The Switch is an activation mechanism purchased from the Mechanic for 20 SC . It can also be found in the Dungeon connected to Chain Lanterns , where it can be harvested with a Pickaxe or Drill .

conditions: The Switch is an activation mechanism purchased from the Mechanic for 20 SC . It can also be found in the Dungeon connected to Chain Lanterns , where it can be harvested with a Pickaxe or Drill .

notes: Normal mode row
Normal mode row
The Tam O' Shanter is a Hardmode vanity item purchased from the Clothier for 2 GC 50 SC , during the third quarter moon phase .

chanceText: (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) 1% / (Old-gen console and 3DS versions) 0.67%
quantityText: Tattered Bee Wing ( (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) Only after one mechanical boss has been defeated (Old-gen console and 3DS versions) At any time)

conditions: The Teal Mushroom is a type of mushroom . It grows on Dirt , Stone , Mud , or regular Jungle grass in the Underground and Cavern layers. [ 1 ] Unlike many plants, instead of broken by any attack, it has to be harvested with a pickaxe . Its sole purpose is to make Teal Dye .

notes: Team Dye is a dye that can be purchased from the Dye Trader while playing Multiplayer . The color of this dye will correspond to the team the player is on; if the player is not on a team, it acts as though no dye was equipped.

conditions: The Teleportation Potion is a potion that teleports the user to a random location. In addition to being crafted, it can be found in Chests located in the Cavern layer and in Shadow Chests .

notes: The Teleporter is a travel mechanism purchased from the (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) Mechanic / (Old-gen console and 3DS versions) Steampunker for 2 GC 50 SC . When two Teleporters are wired together with a triggering mechanism, they can exchange players, NPCs , and most enemies between them. Teleporting is always instantaneous, has no load time or cooldown, no Mana or coin cost, and no limit on distance.

conditions: It is obtainable from the unique background object found in an Enchanted Sword Shrine or very rarely in the Caverns layer. When the object is destroyed, it has a 1/30 (3.33%) chance to drop a Terragrim rather than an Enchanted Sword .

The Destroyer spawns coiled up, like most worm enemies, and immediately starts uncoiling and following the player. It has limited mobility when fully in the air, requiring blocks or platforms to "burrow" through. However, it is able to move freely while off-screen. It follows the player from any distance and will only despawn if the player dies or if dawn comes (4:30 AM). It despawns by quickly traveling underground until off-screen.

notes: Normal mode row
Normal mode row

conditions: The Rotted Fork is a pre-Hardmode spear that has a 1/5 (20%) chance of being obtained from destroying Crimson Hearts found in the Crimson or a 1/5 (20%) chance from Crimson Crates and Hematic Crates . When thrust, it creates a small shockwave at its tip.
Thin Ice is a type of block found in the Snow and Ice biomes . It is found naturally in two configurations: either as a single layer over a pool of water , or in large packed regions underground.

notes: The Throwing Knife is a consumable ranged weapon . It can hit up to 2 targets. It can be purchased from the Merchant during a Blood Moon for 50 CC each and may also be found in Chests . The Throwing Knife is greatly affected by gravity and arcs similarly to Shurikens and Bones . It has the greatest range out of these consumable ranged weapons (with Bones having the least).
sourceRefName: Merchant during a Blood Moon for

conditions: The Storm Spear is a pre-Hardmode spear that can be found in Sandstone Chests in the lower 3/7ths of the Underground Desert and in Oasis Crates and Mirage Crates obtained from fishing in the Oasis . When thrust, it fires an electric bolt that deals 160% of the weapon's damage (21 base damage) and inflicts 50% its knockback (base knockback of 3 (Very weak) ). [ 1 ] The bolt does not pierce enemies and cannot pass through blocks .

The Thunder Zapper is a pre-Hardmode wand that can be found in Sandstone Chests in the lower 3/7ths of the Underground Desert and in Oasis Crates and Mirage Crates obtained from fishing in the Oasis . When used, it fires a lightning bolt that travels in a zigzag pattern horizontally (but traveling straight when shooting directly up or down). The bolt dissipates with a visual explosion after traveling for about one second or upon hitting an enemy or a block .

notes: The Tiki Totem is a Hardmode pet-summoning item that spawns a Tiki Spirit that floats and spins above the player's head. Unlike most pets, it will not teleport to the player when the player moves too quickly for it to keep up; it will simply float straight towards the player. The Tiki Totem can be purchased from the Witch Doctor for 2 PC when he is spoken to in the Jungle biome during Hardmode .

conditions: The Tiki Totem is a Hardmode pet-summoning item that spawns a Tiki Spirit that floats and spins above the player's head. Unlike most pets, it will not teleport to the player when the player moves too quickly for it to keep up; it will simply float straight towards the player. The Tiki Totem can be purchased from the Witch Doctor for 2 PC when he is spoken to in the Jungle biome during Hardmode .

Tin Brick is a type of Brick and an aesthetic building material made from Tin Ore . In some worlds it can also be harvested from Jungle Shrines . Like the ore it is made of, placed Tin Bricks will sparkle. As tin becomes fairly useless early in the game, this is an inexpensive ore brick to build with. Despite being made of Tin , it has no sell value, as with most other blocks. Tin Plating is also available as a decorative block, with the same ingredients and color but a different texture.

The Tin Brick Wall is a type of background wall which can be found naturally generated in Jungle Shrines in some worlds. It drops as an item when destroyed, and can also be obtained via crafting. It is always considered a safe wall .

notes: The Tinkerer's Workshop is a crafting station that is used primarily to combine multiple accessories into single items that usually provide the abilities of their components. For instance, it can combine the Rocket Boots and Hermes Boots into a single item (the Spectre Boots ) that provides the same rocket-jumping and accelerated movement as the individual items do, without occupying two accessory slots. The Tinkerer's Workshop can be purchased from the Goblin Tinkerer for 10 GC .
